proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e` equal to 0.
The strongest `dependency_BRAF` subgroup remains defined by `braf_v600e` equal to 1, with `lineage` adding little beyond the mutation indicator.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_BRAF by braf_v600e.
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1` cell lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.804698 versus -0.249503 in `braf_v600e=0` lines (n=5574 mutated). More negative scores indicate stronger dependency.

analyses.code: Systematic single, pair, and triple predicate screen over binary, categorical, and q25/q75 continuous feature predicates for dependency_BRAF.
analyses.result_summary: The top biologically coherent screen hit was still `braf_v600e=1`; adding `lineage=skin` gave mean -0.811388 in the subgroup and an effect of -0.520456 versus the rest, not materially stronger than the mutation-only effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `egfr_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines without `egfr_amplification`.

Within `egfr_amplification` positive cell lines, high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (top quartile, >=5.669) and low `mutation_burden` (bottom quartile, <=3.230) mark a subgroup with especially negative `dependency_EGFR`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_EGFR by egfr_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.601046 versus -0.252407 in non-amplified lines (n=3880 amplified).

analyses.code: Systematic heterogeneity screen over feature predicates, all compatible pairs, and triples extending top pairs.
analyses.result_summary: The screened subgroup `egfr_amplification=1`, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm>=5.669`, and `mutation_burden<=3.230` had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6714 versus -0.2775 outside the subgroup (n=245).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines without `erbb2_amplification`.
The subgroup with `erbb2_amplification` equal to 1, `lineage` equal to breast, and high `ifn_gamma_signature` (top quartile, >=0.602) has especially negative `dependency_ERBB2`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_ERBB2 by erbb2_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.685595 versus -0.253473 in non-amplified lines (n=4890 amplified).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_ERBB2 heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The `erbb2_amplification=1`, breast, high-`ifn_gamma_signature` subgroup had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.7597 versus -0.2930 outside the subgroup (n=292).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with wild-type `kras_mutation` equal to 0.
Among `kras_mutation` positive lines, the subgroup with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, >=0.964) and `lineage` equal to hematopoietic has especially negative `dependency_KRAS`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KRAS by kras_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.559415 versus -0.251959 in `kras_mutation=0` lines (n=11842 mutated).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_KRAS heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The screened subgroup `kras_mutation=1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964`, and `lineage=hematopoietic` had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.6466 versus -0.3233 outside the subgroup (n=232). This is stronger than the mutation-only average but small enough to treat as exploratory.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with intact `nf1_loss` equal to 0.
The subgroup with `nf1_loss` equal to 1 and `lineage` equal to brain has especially negative `dependency_RIT1` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RIT1 by nf1_loss.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.352716 versus -0.257196 in `nf1_loss=0` lines (n=4560).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_RIT1 heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1` and `lineage=brain` had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.404687 versus -0.264988 outside the subgroup (n=329).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage` equal to colorectal have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.

The complete subgroup `lineage` equal to colorectal, `apc_mutation` equal to 1, and high `wnt_activity_score` (top quartile, >=0.819) has markedly more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores, whereas partial matches to this subgroup do not show the same dependency.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A by colorectal lineage.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.720354 versus -0.252397 in non-colorectal lines (n=5816 colorectal).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_KIF18A heterogeneity screen over feature predicates, compatible pairs, and triples; then eight-cell breakdown for colorectal/APC/high-WNT predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819` subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.111866 versus -0.253014 outside the subgroup (n=3133). In the eight-group breakdown, the other colorectal/APC/high-WNT partial combinations had means near -0.248 to -0.273, supporting the complete three-predicate definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, >=0.964) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
The subgroup with `nf1_loss` equal to 1, high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964), and low `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` (bottom quartile, <=1.956) has more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_TMED10 for top-quartile growth rate versus the rest.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 versus -0.2447 outside the high-growth group (n=12577). The effect was statistically significant but small.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_TMED10 heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The top screened molecular subgroup `nf1_loss=1`, high growth, and low `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm` had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.3556 versus -0.2534 outside the subgroup (n=307). Because univariate molecular effects were weak, this remains exploratory.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (top quartile, >=5.669) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than other cell lines.
The subgroup with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964) and high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (>=5.669) has especially negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_DHX9 by top-quartile rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm.
analyses.result_summary: High-`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3551 versus -0.3265 in the rest (n=12501).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_DHX9 heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The high-growth and high-`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` subgroup had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 versus -0.3297 outside the subgroup (n=3320).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (top quartile, >=0.964) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
The strongest `dependency_POLR2A` heterogeneity is primarily tied to growth-rate and screen covariates rather than a specific oncogenic mutation subgroup.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_POLR2A for top-quartile growth rate versus the rest.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 versus -0.3928 outside the high-growth group (n=12577), the largest non-KIF18A continuous covariate effect observed.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_POLR2A heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The screen repeatedly selected high growth as the leading predicate; examples included high growth with `lineage_subtype=her2_enriched` (effect -0.1174) and high growth with `cas9_activity_score>=1.080` (effect -0.09761). Mutation indicators had much smaller effects, for example `myc_amplification=1` effect -0.01040.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification` equal to 1 or high `copy_number_8q24` (top quartile, >=0.428) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.
The subgroup with `myc_amplification` equal to 1, high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964), and `lineage` equal to pancreatic has especially negative `dependency_RPL11` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparisons of dependency_RPL11 by myc_amplification and top-quartile copy_number_8q24.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.361392 versus -0.252335 in non-amplified lines (effect -0.109056). Top-quartile `copy_number_8q24` also showed a strong negative effect of -0.09875.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_RPL11 heterogeneity screen over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The `myc_amplification=1`, high-growth, pancreatic subgroup had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.4384 versus -0.2690 outside the subgroup (n=268).
